19.reason: This line has a semicolon after the condition, making the break statement unconditional. The break should only execute when n==-1, but due to the semicolon, the break always executes after the if-else chain, causing the program to exit after the first iteration.

The break should only execute when n==-1, but due to the semicolon, the break always executes after the if-else chain, causing the program to exit after the first iteration.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Semicolon after condition makes this an empty statement, so the following break is always executed, causing the loop to exit after the first iteration regardless of input.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon prematurely terminates the if statement, causing the subsequent break; to execute unconditionally and exit the loop immediately.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon after the condition terminates the else-if statement prematurely, causing the break; statement on the next line to execute unconditionally in every loop iteration, which incorrectly terminates the program after processing only the first operation.

20.reason: The semicolon after the else if(n==-1) makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately after reading -1 without processing it correctly. It should be: else if(n==-1) break;

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  The semicolon after the else if(n==-1) makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately after reading -1 without processing it correctly. It should be: else if(n==-1) break;
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The semicolon after 'else if(n==-1)' makes the break unconditional, causing the loop to exit immediately after any non-1 and non-0 input, including -1, but also preventing proper handling of -1 as a termination signal. The break should be inside the else if block without a semicolon.]
